Ad Text:
📊 Data Analyst (PowerBI Specialist)

🌊 Flood Restoration Program | Contract

📍 Location: Lismore (Hybrid / Remote Available)

About the Flood Restoration Team

The Flood Restoration team plays a vital role in supporting communities impacted by natural disasters, delivering large‑scale recovery and restoration programs following flood events. The team works closely across infrastructure, construction, finance, and local government stakeholders to manage complex, fast‑moving initiatives that require strong coordination, transparency, and trusted data.

Data and insights are central to this work - enabling leaders to track progress, manage risk, allocate funding, and make timely, evidence‑based decisions in a highly dynamic environment.

About the Role

We are seeking a highly experienced Data Analyst (PowerBI Specialist) to support the Flood Restoration team by designing and delivering high‑quality reporting and actionable insights. This is a hands‑on, delivery‑focused role, working closely with operational teams and senior stakeholders to transform complex, multi‑source data into clear, intuitive Power BI dashboards that drive decision‑making.

This role would suit someone who has worked in a busy, complex local government environment, or ideally within construction or infrastructure programs with many moving parts and dependencies.

Key Responsibilities

Design, develop, and maintain PowerBI dashboards and reports to support business decision‑making
Build and optimise data models using Power Query (M) and DAX
Integrate data from multiple sources including SQL databases, Excel, SharePoint, APIs, and cloud platforms
Gather, analyse, and translate business requirements into effective data visualisations
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and governance across reports and datasets
Manage PowerBI Service elements including workspaces, datasets, refresh schedules, and row‑level security (RLS)
Collaborate with stakeholders to deliver user‑friendly, insightful reporting solutions
Identify trends, risks, and opportunities through data analysis and storytelling
Maintain documentation and provide user training and reporting support
Continuously improve the performance, usability, and scalability of BI solutions
Adhere to organisational data privacy, security, and compliance standards

About You

Strong hands‑on experience delivering end‑to‑end PowerBI solutions
Advanced skills in DAX, Power Query (M), and data modelling
Experience integrating and reconciling data from multiple systems and sources
Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills
Comfortable working in fast‑paced, evolving environments
Experience in local government, construction, infrastructure, or operational delivery programs is highly desirable
Strong attention to detail with a focus on data integrity and governance

What's on Offer

Competitive contract rates
Hybrid / remote working available
Initial presence in Lismore required for onboarding and stakeholder engagement
Opportunity to contribute to high‑impact flood recovery and restoration initiatives
Work within a collaborative team delivering outcomes that directly support communities

Your new company

Service and Creative Skills Australia (SaCSA) is an industry-owned, industry-led Jobs & Skills Council (JSC), funded by the Department of Employment & Workplace Relations (DEWR). We offer workforce planning, guidance, and advocacy for the following industries:

Arts
Personal Services
Retail
Tourism
Hospitality
Working within a tripartite approach, SaCSA is the critical link between industry stakeholders, government, and unions. We aim to provide a stronger, more strategic voice in ensuring Australia's Vocational Education and Training sector delivers stronger outcomes for employers, workers, learners, trainers, and educators. As the leader in workforce development initiatives for our industries, SaCSA collaborates with industry to identify and articulate current and future employment trends and develops solutions that best serve those needs.

Your new role

The General Manager, Insights & Planning is accountable to the Executive Director, Workforce Strategy and Communications for leading the Insights and Planning function, bringing together workforce data, applied research and analysis, and industry intelligence to shape forward-looking workforce strategies and influence the national skills system.

This role blends labour market and workforce planning expertise with market research capability, ensuring SaCSA's insights are co-designed with industry, analytically rigorous and translated into compelling publications, narratives, and evidence-based recommendations for the industries within SaCSA's remit, government and the broader public.
Key outcomes:

Brings together people leadership, applied research, data stewardship and strategic translation, operating at the intersection of industry co-design, quantitative and qualitative analysis, and forward-looking strategy development to ensure SaCSA's insights are credible, timely, relevant and actively used.

Provides strong, visible and empowering leadership to the Insights and Planning team, fostering a high-performing culture grounded in curiosity, collaboration, and analytical rigour.
Oversees the end-to-end development of research projects, publications, workforce planning strategies, and insights products, from research through to delivery and dissemination.


What you'll need to succeed

Demonstrated ability to lead and develop multidisciplinary research or analytics teams.
Demonstrated experience working with key workforce and occupational data models and classification frameworks (e.g. ANZSCO, OSCA and related industry datasets), with the ability to apply these in analysis, interpretation, submissions, and insight development.
Significant experience in applied research, labour market, workforce analysis, or related fields.
Strong understanding of qualitative and quantitative research methodologies.
Experience working within, or in close partnership with relevant government, statutory bodies, or policy-focused organisations (JSA, ABS, NCVER, etc).

What you need to do now

AEMO at the Heart of Energy   

AEMO is Australia’s independent energy system and market operator and system planner. AEMO’s purpose is to ensure safe, reliable and affordable energy today, and enable the energy transition for the benefit of all Australians.  Australian governments are now committed to a net zero economy for Australia by 2050 and our vision is to enable this. We have the once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to co-design the future of our energy systems, and our core values revolve around character, commitment, and connection. Join us as we contribute to this significant transition in the energy sector.    

About the role

The WA Market Reforms team sits within the Western Australia division and is responsible for the working with the Western Australian Government to define and implement policy change to the Western Australian Wholesale Electricity Market (WEM) and WA Gas Bulletin Board. 

You will work autonomously to lead the analysis, design and implementation of complex process and system changes to execute the WA Governments energy policy across AEMOs systems and processes. As a subject matter authority on energy settlement practices, you will provide expert advice to the WA Government to support the development of rule changes and lead the subsequent implementation of the policy within AEMOs systems and processes. 

What you will be doing 

Work with AEMO teams and WA Government to contribute expert advice on mathematical or process implementation to design complex market enhancement and/or process improvements through regulatory change.

Leverage subject matter expertise of markets, systems and processes to proactively identify and manage complex technical, operational, regulatory and policy risks across metering, settlement, prudentials, and retail market functions, supported by robust quantitative and qualitative analysis.

Lead the identification and analysis of changes to procedures, processes and/or systems resulting from regulatory change, including elaborating settlement policy as mathematical formula.

Provide direction to projects during feasibility and support project resources during implementation by providing expert understanding of the ESM and GSI rules, mechanics of electricity and gas systems, and a technical understanding of AEMOs systems and processes.

Coach and supporting team members to develop expert technical advice and business acumen on complex cross functional technical, operational, regulatory or policy issues affecting AEMOs functions.

What you will bring to the role  

Experienced energy sector professional with a comprehensive understanding of the WA energy markets and power systems and demonstrated experience in project delivery of IT systems and process.

Significant experience in a commercial or regulatory environment, ideally but not limited to experience in the Energy sector, and experience interpreting and operating in compliance with technical documents and/or legal instruments, such as rules and regulations.

Proven qualitative and quantitative analytical skills, with the ability to deal with a range of complex issues concurrently, propose technically resourceful, evidenced based solutions to issues and execute solutions in a constructive and timely manner.

Proficient expertise with developing mathematical equations and notations using markup languages, like LaTeX.

Experience related to metering, prudentials and settlements in the WEM is highly desirable.

In return some of our benefits to you 

Flexible working: work from home, part time, job share, hybrid options, and additional leave options 

Professional development via projects, industry networks, job rotation, study assistance and more. 

Give back with up to 4 days of volunteering leave per year.  

Reward your hard work with annual performance bonuses (subject to eligibility). 

Embrace a healthier you with our wellness program, discounted health insurance, gym perks and our comprehensive Employee Assistance Program (EAP).  

About Our Process  

AEMO values diversity and inclusivity in the workplace, welcoming applications from all backgrounds without regard to age, disability, gender, sexual orientation, parental status, race, religion and our First Nation peoples. We are dedicated to accommodating applicants' needs during the application or interview process, simply let us know by emailing tal•••@aemo.com.au.

Job Title: Lecturer / Senior Lecturer in Quantitative Finance
Ad Text:
The School of Mathematical and Physical Sciences warmly invites applications from underrepresented groups including women, Indigenous Australians, people with visible and non-visible disability, neurodiverse individuals, those who identify as LGBTQIA+ and applicants from diverse cultural and linguistic backgrounds. UTS was one of the first Australian institutions to receive the Athena SWAN Bronze Accreditation for our commitment to action addressing gender equity in the STEMM disciplines. We’re actively creating a more inclusive and supportive community through initiatives like Flexible Working, Wingara Indigenous Employment Strategy, LGBTQIA+ community support and the UTS Access and Inclusion Plan.




About the role

At the UTS School of Mathematical and Physical Sciences, we do research that matters, in an industry-oriented, multidisciplinary environment, linking fundamental research to practical problems. The School deliver's research which has impact on these major challenges facing society, together with educated and adaptable students who can contribute to their solution in a collaborative program of engagement with employers, industry and government.

As a Lecturer / Senior Lecturer in Quantitative Finance, you will contribute to teaching and program development with a focus on postgraduate degrees. You will deliver high-quality teaching, coordinate subjects, and supervise students, while leading innovative subject and course development.

You will maintain an active research profile, producing high-quality outputs and addressing applied research challenges. The role also involves building strong collaborations with industry, government, and academic partners to enhance the impact of your discipline.




About You

You are a strong communicator with excellent interpersonal with the ability to engage effectively with diverse stakeholders. You demonstrate research leadership and the ability to build collaborative partnerships across quantitative finance and related fields. You work both independently and as part of a team, contributing positively to shared goals. Other key selection criteria for this role include:

PhD in a field relevant to the research scope of the position.

Demonstrated ability to teach  quantitative finance and the ability or potential to develop high quality learning resources for different delivery modes.

Demonstrated and nationally recognised track record of research excellence with potential to secure nationally competitive research grants.

Ability to provide supervision for Higher Degree Postgraduate and Honours students.

Demonstrated involvement in the wider academic and professional community.




What We Offer

Base Salary Range:  $125,240 - $148,229 (Level B) / $152,245 - $174,841 (Level C) plus 17% superannuation (pension).

The position is full-time and will be made on a continuing basis.

UTS staff also benefit from a wide range of Employee Benefits include flexible work practices, generous parental leave and salary packaging opportunities.




How To Apply

Please include the following in your application and ensure all documents submitted include reference number IRC337384 and your name.

a cover letter stating your motivation for applying.

a copy of your resume.

responses to the selection criteria in the ‘Position Description’.




UTS prioritises safety, respect, and inclusion for every member of our community. In support of this, and in line with our statutory obligations under the National Code to Prevent and Respond to Gender-Based Violence 2026, all prospective employees must declare any past investigations relating to gender-based violence. Employment at UTS is contingent upon completion of this declaration, and, where necessary, assessment of any identified risk factors. We appreciate your role in upholding the standards and behaviour expected of our community.




General enquiries or issues with your application may be directed to Marie Hetherington in the UTS Recruitment Team at recruitment@uts.edu.au.

Closing Date: Monday, 1st June 2026 at 11.59 pm (AEST).

We welcome international applicants!




Accessibility

UTS Recruitment is committed to digital accessibility. If you experience any difficulty with the content of our advertising, please let us know so we can ensure our information is accessible to everyone.

In addition, if you have a disability that requires adjustment to the recruitment process or an alternative application pathway, please contact recruitment@uts.edu.au for assistance.

About Dentons

Dentons is the world's largest law firm. We're 20,000+ people globally across 80+ countries including USA; Europe; India and Asia. We are a global polycentric firm which is expanding rapidly in an increasingly globalised legal industry.

Dentons now consistently ranks as one of the five best regarded legal brands in the world according to the Acritas Global Elite Law Firm Brand Index.







About the Role


Dentons currently has an exciting opportunity for an innovative and detail-oriented Business Analyst to join our team on a 12-month contract.


As a Business Analyst, you will be responsible for enhancing the quality of our technology products by analysing business needs and opportunities. You will work closely with Organisational Change Management and Technical SMEs to design improvements, facilitate business testing, and support implementation. This role will see you working on Dentons' Case Management System project, with experience in ShareDo (Clio Operate), Appian, or other CRMs highly regarded.

Requirements gathering sessions to capture business needs, including risk identification in partnership with project managers.

Working with project managers to develop and implement plans for successful project delivery, improvements and new solutions.

Participate in testing with particular emphasis on UAT, Business Pre & Post Implementation Verification.
Process mapping with focus on automation
Maintain and enhance documentation repositories and project tools (e.g., project plans, dashboards, knowledge bases).

What You'll Need to Succeed

3-5 years experience as a Business Analyst or similar role, working in the legal industry
Agile Business Analyst, BABOK or related Certification.
User Acceptance, Regression and Business Verification Testing.
Proficiency in a variety of Project tools/systems (e.g. Microsoft Excel, Smartsheet, PowerPoint, SharePoint, Teams, PowerBI, Jira/Confluence etc).
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to foster relationships across all levels of the business.
Proven analytical mindset, capable of solving complex problems and presenting actionable solutions.

Why Dentons

Dentons Australia offers a wide range of incentives aimed at embracing your authenticity, providing purpose, and strengthening your ongoing development.

Diversity, Equality & Inclusion

Job Title: Technical Data and AI Product Manager
Ad Text:
Join a SaaS company that provides an AI-powered, cloud-based platform for builders and contractors to instantly assess property feasibility. The company services client globally. This is a fully remote position. 

About The Role
We’re looking for a Technical Data & AI Product Manager to join a fast growing SaaS company. This role is ideal for someone who can connect business needs, data opportunities, and emerging AI capabilities to deliver scalable, high-impact solutions.

You’ll work at the intersection of data engineering, AI/GenAI, and product strategy, defining what we should build, why it matters, and how it integrates into our platform. You’ll collaborate closely with engineers, data specialists, and other Product Managers to shape the future of the organisations AI-powered features and data-driven insights.

The ideal candidate has strong technical understanding (data pipelines, APIs, AI model capabilities), hands-on product experience, and the ability to bring structure and clarity in a fast-paced, evolving startup environment.

Key Responsibilities

Define and own the roadmap for data and AI/GenAI initiatives, from early discovery through delivery.
Translate business and user needs into data and AI-driven solutions, framing problems and hypotheses clearly.
Envision AI and GenAI use cases across company ecosystem — from automations to user-facing intelligence — and scope MVPs or spikes to validate feasibility.
Collaborate with data engineers to define and prioritize data pipelines, models, and infrastructure improvements.
Work closely with data analysts to ensure relevant KPIs, dashboards, and insights are delivered to stakeholders.
Partner with other Product Managers to understand business flows, identify where data sits, and ensure consistent, reliable data usage across the platform.
Partner with engineering and design to translate AI and data opportunities into user-centric features.
Define and monitor success metrics for AI and data products — ensuring outcomes are measurable and traceable to business impact.
Write clear user stories and technical requirements for data and AI-related projects.
Balance experimentation and delivery, ensuring short-term wins while building toward long-term AI maturity.
Communicate clearly across technical and non-technical stakeholders, simplifying complex ideas and progress updates.
Take strong ownership of your domain, working autonomously while keeping the broader team aligned.


Requirements

4+ years of Product Management experience, ideally in SaaS or technology environments.
Proven experience working with data, analytics, or AI/ML/GenAI initiatives in a product or technical capacity.
Strong understanding of data architecture concepts (data pipelines, APIs, integrations, SQL, ETL/ELT processes).
Experience collaborating with data engineers and analysts on delivering data products or AI features.
Solid grasp of AI and GenAI capabilities (LLMs, embeddings, retrieval, automation, NLP, etc.) and ability to scope practical use cases.
Comfort driving spikes and proofs-of-concept to validate technical feasibility before committing to full builds.
Excellent skills in problem framing, hypothesis testing, and prioritization.
Strong ability to self-organize, take ownership, and communicate effectively under pressure.
Experience working closely with engineering and design teams in Agile environments.
Proactive and pragmatic — balances innovation with feasibility.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
If interested, APPLY NOW for more details.

Job Title: Senior Consultant, Asset Management and Performance
Ad Text:
Want to bring your consulting skills into engineering and asset management?

At Aurecon, we're an Australian-owned design, engineering and advisory company with a proud history of helping clients manage their assets across the full lifecycle. With strong growth across Australia, we're seeking a Consultant to join our national Asset Management Advisory team.



This is an opportunity to apply structured problem solving and technical analysis to some of the most complex decision-making challenges facing asset-intensive organisations - from infrastructure and utilities to energy and industry. You'll work alongside our eminent engineers, combining rigorous analysis with practical consulting skills to help clients make better decisions about their assets.




How you'll make an impact

You'll work with both public and private sector clients, contributing to projects that drive operational excellence and sustainable outcomes. You'll lead packages of work within larger multi-disciplinary projects, taking ownership of your workstream from analysis through to recommendations - and building the client relationships that go with it.




Your work will include:
Leading or being a critical team member for delivery of multi-disciplinary projects, coordinating inputs across technical disciplines and delivering clear, evidence-based outputs.
Applying structured analytical approaches to support complex decision making for asset-intensive organisations - investment prioritisation, performance improvement, risk and reliability.
Translating technical data and insights into clear advice and practical decisions for clients.
Contributing to solutions in areas such as asset management strategies, performance improvement, system design and asset condition assessments.
Working alongside Aurecon's engineers and technical specialists, bridging the gap between deep technical expertise and client-facing consulting outcomes.

What you'll bring:
A degree in Engineering, Science, Technology or a related discipline - or equivalent experience that gives you a technical lens on problems.
Around 3-5 years of experience in consulting, engineering, operations or a technical advisory role.
Strong problem-solving and analytical skills, with the ability to turn insights into outcomes.
The ability to work with data - interpreting outputs, identifying patterns and turning analysis into clear recommendations.
Clear and confident communication skills, with the ability to engage a wide range of stakeholders.
Financial and commercial acumen, or a demonstrated interest in developing it quickly - industry experience in this area is highly regarded.
A collaborative, proactive approach and the confidence to lead your own workstreams while drawing on the expertise around you.
Comfort working on site and a willingness to travel occasionally for project work - our clients operate real assets in real places, and getting on the ground is part of how we do our best work.

Nice to have:
Software literacy - comfortable writing basic Python for data analysis, or able to pick up new tools and software quickly to deliver project work more efficiently.



Experience in areas such as strategy, operations, asset performance, or sustainability (including decarbonisation) will be highly regarded, but not essential. We're more interested in how you think than in ticking specific boxes.




Why join us?
Impact - help organisations make informed, long-term decisions that shape the future of communities and industries.
Depth - work shoulder-to-shoulder with some of Australia's most experienced engineers and advisors.
Growth - develop both technical and consulting skills, with opportunities to move into client engagement and business development.
Culture - join an inclusive team where your voice will be heard, and where we're recognised for gender equality, innovation and diversity.
Australian pride - work for a privately owned business with a global outlook and a strong local footprint.

Ready to bring ideas to life?

Apply today and help us shape what's next in Asset Management.



Important Note: As part of our recruitment process, you may be contacted via phone, email or SMS. Please be assured that any messages you receive from us are legitimate and directly related to your application. If you're ever unsure, you're welcome to contact us directly to confirm.

The Byrnecut Group

With over 6,000 employees, the Byrnecut Group is a full-service underground mining contractor, providing services to major mine owners throughout Australia and Internationally. We operate a multi-million-dollar fleet of specialised underground mining equipment across multiple continents and conditions.

Green Light Australia are partnering with a leading company to engage a Senior Cyber Security Analyst to drive threat intelligence capabilities, enhance cyber defence posture, and provide actionable insights into emerging threats. This role will play a critical part in identifying, analysing, and operationalising cyber threat intelligence to support security operations, incident response, and risk management across the organisation.

Responsibilities:

Threat Intelligence & Adversary Analysis
Research, analyse, and profile threat actors, including motivations, capabilities, infrastructure, and targeting patterns.
Map adversary tactics, techniques, and procedures (TTPs) across the attack lifecycle using frameworks such as MITRE ATT&CK.
Produce strategic, operational, and tactical intelligence reports tailored to SOC, engineering teams, and senior leadership.
Intelligence Collection & Analysis
Collect and analyse intelligence from multiple sources including OSINT, commercial feeds, internal telemetry, and incident data.
Correlate data to identify trends, patterns, and emerging threats relevant to the organisation.
Manage the intelligence lifecycle including requirements, collection, analysis, dissemination, and feedback.
Assess intelligence quality, confidence levels, and relevance to organisational risk.
Threat Modelling & Risk Contextualisation
Apply threat modelling techniques to assess potential impacts to the organisation.
Identify gaps in detection and prevention controls using intelligence insights.
Translate threat intelligence into risk-based prioritisation and actionable recommendations.
Detection Engineering & SOC Integration
Integrate threat intelligence into SIEM use cases, detection rules, and alerting mechanisms.
Collaborate with SOC, detection engineering, and threat hunting teams to operationalise intelligence.
Enrich incidents and alerts with contextual intelligence, including attacker intent and potential next steps.
Technical Security Expertise
Apply knowledge of network protocols, endpoint security, identity systems, and cloud environments.
Analyse common attack techniques such as phishing, malware, credential abuse, and lateral movement.
Utilise security tools including SIEM, EDR, NDR, and threat intelligence platforms.
Operational & Strategic Contributions
Identify threat actors relevant to the organisation and monitor associated activity.
Integrate threat intelligence feeds into security platforms and automate detection workflows.
Develop and maintain playbooks, processes, and frameworks to mature the threat intelligence capability.
Provide intelligence support during active incidents and investigations.
Identify and contextualise critical and zero-day vulnerabilities for vulnerability management teams.
Develop metrics and reporting to enhance situational awareness of the threat landscape.
Collaboration & Stakeholder Engagement
Work closely with SOC, Red Team, Vulnerability Management, and Incident Response teams.
Communicate complex technical threats clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Support data breach investigations and third-party risk monitoring.
Requirements

Strong understanding of threat intelligence frameworks, especially MITRE ATT&CK.
Proven experience in threat intelligence analysis within enterprise or financial services environments.
Hands-on experience with SIEM, EDR, threat intelligence platforms, and security tooling.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail.
Experience producing high-quality intelligence reports and briefings.

We are an Australian onshore services company that provides technology capability across ANZ and Asia. Our services range from day-to-day operational activities, through project delivery, to more strategic and transformational programs. Job Title: Multiple Azure & Microsoft Fabric Analytics Contractors

Artificial Intelligence Specialist

Do you want to protect future generations?

Protect tomorrow together at the NSW EPA

Join a purpose-led organisation and make a difference to the environment and our community
Work on issues that matter in a supportive, collaborative and creative environment
Environment Officer Class 11
Remuneration starting from $144,018 plus superannuation
Benefits and flexibility recognising work isn’t all you are passionate about
One ongoing role available
Role based in Parramatta
The opportunity

The Artificial Intelligence Specialist leads the design, development and implementation of AI-driven solutions that enhance the EPA’s regulatory, operational and environmental intelligence capabilities. The role supports the agency’s digital transformation by applying advanced analytics, machine learning and automation to improve environmental monitoring, compliance, decision-making and service delivery.

About you

To be successful in this role, you will have the following key knowledge and experience:

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to assess data quality, identify patterns, and propose evidence-based solutions.
Demonstrated experience designing, developing, training, evaluating and deploying machine learning and artificial intelligence models to solve complex business or operational problems.
Proficiency in programming languages and tools relevant to machine learning and data analysis (e.g. Python, R, TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn, SQL).
Experience working with large and diverse datasets, including data processing, feature engineering, model optimisation and validation.
Experience applying cloud-based and/or on-premise machine learning platforms and deployment tools (e.g. AWS, Azure, GCP, containerisation technologies).
Ability to communicate complex technical concepts, model outputs and data insights clearly and effectively to technical and non-technical stakeholders

Also, it is an essential requirement of this role for the successful candidate to hold a degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Software Engineering, Mathematics, or related discipline, or equivalent professional experience.

About us

As the environmental steward and regulator of our State we are committed to a sustainable future.

Our people are proud to play a role in protecting the environment of NSW. We partner with Aboriginal people, industry and the wider community to protect, restore and enhance our diverse ecosystems.

We bring scientific expertise, experience and actions to inspire innovative solutions as we transition our state to a circular economy.

As we respond to urgent current issues, including climate change, we educate and support our stakeholders to create an environmentally healthy future.

Join us on our mission to protect tomorrow together.

Why work for us?

EPA people are energised by our purpose and focussed on the environment and we invite all our team members to:

Be part of protecting what matters – be purpose led making a meaningful impact to the environment, economy and communities delivering for a better NSW
Do more and be inspired together – be members of passionate united teams who protect the environment and each other through care, trust and collaboration.
Bring who you are and grow with us - we put people first creating a culture where everyone can be themselves, respecting and developing our diverse skills & aspirations and prioritising our safety and wellbeing.

Some additional benefits to working for us include:

A competitive salary and leave entitlements, salary packaging to save tax, fitness passport and free & confidential counselling services
Opportunities and pathways for learning and developing your skills with access to online and face to face training and mentoring
Flexible work arrangements
Opportunities to make an impact and help shape the future for our environment
Interesting and challenging work aligning with personal values that allow you to reach your full potential
Recognition programs

In our inclusive and accessible workplace, differences are embraced, contributions are valued, and everyone has a sense of connection and belonging. The EPA supports various employee affinity groups, practice flexible working, offer job share and workplace adjustments.

The EPA is an independent statutory authority which sits within the NSW Department of Climate Change, Energy, the Environment and Water (DCCEEW)

For more information on the work we do at NSW EPA, go to www.epa.nsw.gov.au/your-environment

To apply

If excited by the information above, we look forward to receiving your application, including a copy of your resume (max. 5 pages) and cover letter (max. 1 page) expressing your interest and suitability for the role.

As part of your application, you will be required to provide a response (max. 300 words) to the following question:

Describe a time where you designed, deployed or advised on an AI solution. What technologies models or tools did you use and why did you select these? What steps did you take to ensure the solution was ethical transparent explainable and compliant?
